title: Basilica of Bom Jesus
tagline: Basilica of Bom Jesus — spiritual town of Goa

overview.short: The Basilica of Bom Jesus (Portuguese: Basílica do Bom Jesus; Goan Konkani: Borea Jezuchi Bajilika) is a Catholic basilica in Goa, in the Konkan region of India.
overview.description: The Basilica of Bom Jesus (Portuguese: Basílica do Bom Jesus; Goan Konkani: Borea Jezuchi Bajilika) is a Catholic basilica in Goa, in the Konkan region of India. The church is a pilgrimage centre and recognised by UNESCO as a World Heritage Site. The basilica is located in Old Goa, the former capital of Portuguese India, and holds the body of St Francis Xavier. Bom Jesus (meaning, "Good Jesus" in Portuguese) is the name used for the Ecce Homo in Portuguese speaking countries.

topPlaces.name: Churches and convents of Goa
topPlaces.description: Churches and Convents of Goa is the name given by UNESCO to a set of religious monuments located in Goa Velha (or Old Goa), in the state of Goa, India, which were declared a World Heritage Site in 1986. Goa was the capital of Portuguese India and Asia and an evangelization center from the 16th century.

topPlaces.name: Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Goa and Daman
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Metropolitan Archdiocese of Goa and Daman (Latin: Archidioecesis Goanae et Damanensis, Goan Konkani: Gõy ani Damanv Mha-Dhormprant, Portuguese: Arquidiocese de Goa e Damão) encompasses the Goa state and the Damaon territory in the Konkan region, by the west coast of India. The ecclesiastical province of Goa and Damaon includes a suffragan diocese, the Sindhudurg Diocese that comprises the Malvani areas of (central Konkan).

image.alt: Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Goa and Daman

topPlaces.name: Archaeological Museum and Portrait Gallery
topPlaces.description: Archaeological Museum and Portrait Gallery was set up in 1964, NS reorganised in 1981–82. It is run by the Government of India's Archaeological Survey of India and is situated in the former Portuguese colonial capital of Old Goa, a historic one-time city which now attracts a large number of tourists.

topPlaces.name: Old Goa
topPlaces.description: Old Goa is a historical site and city situated on the southern banks of the River Mandovi, within the Tiswadi taluka (Ilhas) of North Goa district, in the Indian state of Goa. The city was established by the Bijapur Sultanate in the 15th century AD. After the Portuguese conquest of Goa, it served as capital of Portuguese Indian possessions, such as Mumbai/Bombay (Bom Bahia) territory and the state of Kochi/Cochin (Cochim), until its abandonment in the 18th century AD due to a plague.

topPlaces.name: Church and Convent of St. Francis of Assisi
topPlaces.description: The Church of St. Francis of Assisi was built in 1661 by the Portuguese in the Portuguese Viceroyalty of India.

image.alt: Church and Convent of St. Francis of Assisi

topPlaces.name: Pelourinho Novo
topPlaces.description: The Pelourinho Novo (lit. 'New Pillory'), also known as Hath Katro Khamb (lit. 'Hand-Cutting Pillar'), is a sixteenth-century black basalt pillory in Old Goa, located in the Indian coastal state of Goa. It acted as a symbolic civic marker and a focal point for official proceedings, where authorities handled legal matters, issued formal announcements, and enforced penalties, including corporal punishment such as the whipping of offenders.

topPlaces.name: Se Cathedral
topPlaces.description: The Sé Catedral de Santa Catarina, known as Se Cathedral, is the cathedral of the Latin Church Archdiocese of Goa and Daman and the seat of the Patriarch of the East Indies. It is part of the World Heritage Site, Churches and convents of Goa located in Old Goa, India.

topPlaces.name: Museum of Christian Art
topPlaces.description: The Museum of Christian Art is an Indian museum of Christian art, located in the Convent of Santa Monica in the former colonial capital of Old Goa, Goa.

'Hand-Cutting Pillar'), is a sixteenth-century black basalt pillory in Old Goa, located in the Indian coastal state of Goa. It acted as a symbolic civic marker and a focal point for official proceedings, where authorities handled legal matters, issued formal announcements, and enforced penalties, including corporal punishment such as the whipping of offenders.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Basilica of Bom Jesus?
faq.a: The best time to visit Basilica of Bom Jesus is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Basilica of Bom Jesus?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Goa International Airport, Dabolim (16 km) and the nearest railway station is Vasco da Gama (Goa) (15 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Basilica of Bom Jesus?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Basilica of Bom Jesus at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Basilica of Bom Jesus?
faq.a: Stays in Basilica of Bom Jesus start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Basilica of Bom Jesus?
faq.a: The top attractions are Churches and convents of Goa, Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Goa and Daman, Archaeological Museum and Portrait Gallery, Old Goa, Church and Convent of St. Francis of Assisi.
